title: Why These 2 Pizza Stocks May Be About to Rise
text: Like many quick service restaurant (QSR) operators, pizza places have struggled in recent months. Cash-strapped consumers are spending less on pricier takeout food and delivery.

title: Domino's (DPZ) Boosts Digital Offerings With Pinpoint Delivery
text: Domino's (DPZ) emphasizes on technological innovation to drive growth. The company unveils a new Pinpoint Delivery service in the U.S. market.

title: Domino's Takes Tip From 7-Eleven With Pinned-Location Delivery
text: As Domino's looks for ways to retain delivery customers' loyalty in the face of increasing competition from aggregators' pizza offerings, the chain is making it possible to get food brought to more types of locations, taking a page from 7-Eleven‘s book.

title: Domino's rolls out new service to deliver pizzas to places like beaches and parks
text: Domino's Pizza unveiled its Pinpoint Delivery service that makes it easier for customers to order deliveries to locations lacking a standard address.

title: Domino's will deliver your next pizza without an address
text: An address is typically required when getting food delivered. Not for Domino's.

title: Markets Ended the Day Lower, but Up for the Week on Inflation, Fed Optimism
text: Markets closed lower for the day, higher for the week on lower inflation, expectations the Fed may be pause or halt interest rate hikes.

title: Markets Little Changed Midday but on Track for Weekly Gains Heading into Holiday
text: U.S. equities were little changed at midday on Friday, June 16, 2023, but on track for a winning week heading into the long holiday weekend.

title: Domino's® Announces Q2 2023 Earnings Webcast
text: ANN ARBOR, Mich., June 16, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- Domino's Pizza, Inc. (NYSE: DPZ) announces the following event: What:  Domino's Second Quarter 2023 Earnings Webcast When:  Monday, July 24 at 8:30 a.m.

title: Enthusiasm for Tech Stocks and Optimism About Fed Rate Hikes Lift Markets
text: Investors' enthusiasm for tech stocks and optimism that the Fed will slow rate hikes lifts markets.

title: Domino's says it's open to apps delivering its pizza. Some analysts think it will bring in the dough.
text: For the past several years, Domino's Pizza Inc. executives have railed against rival U.S. food-delivery apps like DoorDash Inc. DASH, Grubhub and Uber Eats. The apps were losing money, and their aggressive free-delivery and discount offers to customers were unsustainable, they said.

title: 3 Stocks Making Moves After Analyst Notes
text: Analysts are busy weighing in today , and the three stocks below are making bigger-than-usual moves following updates from some of Wall Street's biggest firms.

title: Why Domino's Pizza Stock Is Piping Hot on Thursday
text: Investment bank Stifel upgraded shares of Domino's Pizza to buy last night. Underperforming since the pandemic ended, Stifel sees sales stabilizing soon for the pizza company.

title: Markets Rise Midday as Tech Stocks Climb Amid Rate Hike Optimism
text: U.S. equities advanced at midday on June 15, 2023 as tech stocks climbed after the Fed paused rate hikes.

title: Domino's stock upgraded to buy by Stifel, citing growth potential
text: Domino's Pizza Inc.'s DPZ stock was upgraded to buy from hold by Stifel Thursday, with analysts citing the fast-food chain's growth potential. “Our upgrade reflects our belief that over the next 12 months, the company will stabilize delivery sales and continue growing carryout sales to new record levels,” wrote Stifel analyst Chris O'Cull, in a note.

title: Dominos Pizza: Historic P/E Contraction
text: Domino's Pizza stock is trading at decade-low multiples of earnings, making it an attractive buy for investors. DPZ has a consistent history of EPS growth and strong margins, as well as a healthy balance sheet with significant buybacks. Risks include potential impacts from student debt repayment on consumer spending and the possibility of being re-priced as a stalled growth story.

title: 2 Cheap Stocks That Could Help You Retire Early
text: Coca-Cola and Domino's both possess tremendous brand power. This should lead to continued, solid growth in the years ahead.

title: Domino's Pizza: A Bargain Despite Temporary Margin Compression
text: Domino's Pizza stock has experienced a 26.7% decline from its average price in 2022, presenting an opportune time for investors to consider adding it to their portfolio. The company has demonstrated resilience and growth throughout its history, even during periods of high inflation, and has a strong business model with competitive advantages. Investors should monitor the margins of the supply chain segment and franchise EBITDA, but the current price offers an attractive entry point for long-term investors.

title: U.S. Wide-Moat Stocks On Sale - The June 2023 Heat Map
text: Our 3-step process focuses on wide-moat stocks (as per Morningstar's rating). We are only interested in those targets that are attractively valued in historical comparison. We share the heat map of the most investable candidates that may be worth your time for further analysis.

title: My Top 15 High Growth Dividend Stocks For June 2023
text: I present my top 15 high-growth dividend stocks to consider during the month of June. The watchlist underperformed VIG in May, but continues to outpace the ETF year-to-date and since inception. Five new stocks have been added to the watchlist for June: Intuit, Mastercard, Microchip Technology Inc., Sirius XM Holdings, and Skyworks Solutions.

title: Domino's Pizza: A Dividend Growth Machine At An Attractive Valuation
text: Domino's Pizza shares have been on a downward slope for the last couple of years now as the positive impact of the pandemic wore off. However, we've now gotten to the point where shares look attractively valued for a long-term investor. This is a shareholder-friendly company that has focused on dividends and buybacks.`.
